q.en: According to the book, which months hold the most holiday excitement?
q.zh: 根据本书，哪段时间节日气氛最浓厚？

options.en: The time between November and March
options.zh: 十一月至次年三月之间

options.en: The first days of every new year
Only the single month of December
The summer months of June through August

explain.en: The introduction says there is no busier holiday time than the season between November and March.
explain.zh: 引言提到，十一月到次年三月之间是没有比这更热闹的节日季节了。

q.en: What does the word 'traditions' mean in this book?
q.zh: 本书中 'traditions'（传统）一词是什么意思？

options.en: Rules written down by the government
Things people do because those before them did the same things
options.zh: 人们因为前人这样做而延续下来的事物

options.en: Foods that are only eaten at night
New activities invented every single year according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 每年新发明的活动的活动（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book explains traditions are things people do because the people before them did those same things, passed down through generations.
explain.zh: 书中解释，传统是前人做过、后人也延续去做的事情，代代相传。

q.en: During the daytime of Ramadan, what do Muslims who fast do?
q.zh: 在斋月的白天，封斋的穆斯林会怎么做？

options.en: They eat with their enemies
They do not eat or drink during the day
They eat a large meal at noon according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 中午吃一顿大餐（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They drink only fruit juice

explain.en: The book states Muslims who fast do not eat or drink during the day, breaking the fast at sunset with dates.
explain.zh: 书中说封斋的穆斯林白天不吃不喝，日落后以枣子开斋。

q.en: Both Hanukkah and Kwanzaa involve lighting candles. Which statement is true?
q.zh: 犹太教光明节和宽扎节都有点蜡烛的环节。哪一项是正确的？

options.en: Both holidays use exactly the same candle holder according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 两个节日用的是完全相同的烛台（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Kwanzaa uses a menorah with 9 candles
options.zh: 宽扎节用有9支蜡烛的 menorah

options.en: Hanukkah uses a menorah with 9 candles; Kwanzaa uses a kinara
options.zh: 光明节用有9支蜡烛的 menorah；宽扎节用 kinara

options.en: Neither holiday uses any candles at all
options.zh: 两个节日都不使用蜡烛

explain.en: Hanukkah lights a menorah (9 candles) and Kwanzaa lights candles in a kinara; they are different holders.
explain.zh: 光明节点燃 menorah（9支蜡烛），宽扎节在 kinara 中点燃蜡烛，两者烛台不同。

q.en: How many candles does a Hanukkah menorah hold?
q.zh: 光明节的 menorah（烛台）一共有多少支蜡烛？

options.en: Nine — one for each night plus the shamash
options.zh: 九支——每晚一支，外加一支 shamash

options.en: Seven, one for each day of the week according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 七支，对应一周七天（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Twelve, one for each month
Three, for the three main foods
options.zh: 三支，对应三种主要食物

explain.en: A menorah holds nine candles — one for each of the eight nights and the taller shamash used to light them.
explain.zh: menorah 有九支蜡烛——八夜各一支，外加较高的 shamash 用于点燃其他蜡烛。

q.en: Why does the author say all holidays, no matter the place or faith, have much in common?
q.zh: 作者为什么说无论地点或信仰为何，所有节日都有许多共同点？

options.en: They all happen on the exact same calendar day according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它们都在完全相同的日历日举行（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They all require people to travel far from home
options.zh: 它们都要求人们远行离家

options.en: They all use the color blue and white
They all celebrate family, friends, sharing, and love
options.zh: 它们都庆祝家人、朋友、分享与爱

explain.en: The conclusion states all holidays celebrate family, friends, sharing, and love, which is the author's main point.
explain.zh: 结语指出所有节日都庆祝家人、朋友、分享与爱，这是作者想表达的核心。

q.en: What is the biggest celebration of Chinese New Year, held on the fifteenth night?
q.zh: 中国新年最盛大的庆祝活动在第十五天夜晚举行，它是什么？

options.en: A fast with no food or water
Lighting nine candles in a kinara according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 在 kinara 中点燃九支蜡烛（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A quiet reading of the Koran
Fireworks, the Lion Dance, and the Dragon Parade

explain.en: The book says the biggest celebration comes on the fifteenth night with fireworks, the Lion Dance, and the Dragon Parade.
explain.zh: 书中说第十五夜是最盛大的庆祝，有烟花、舞狮和舞龙游行。

q.en: When is the Hindu holiday Holi celebrated?
q.zh: 印度教节日胡里节（Holi）在什么时候庆祝？

options.en: During the ninth month of the Islamic calendar according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 在伊斯兰历的第九个月（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: In early March, on the first day after the full moon
options.zh: 三月初，满月后的第一天

options.en: Only once every ten years
On December 25 every year

explain.en: Holi is celebrated during the first part of March on the first day after the full moon.
explain.zh: 胡里节在三月上旬、满月后的第一天庆祝。

q.en: Which TWO of these holidays use lighted candles as a central activity?
q.zh: 以下哪两个节日都以点燃蜡烛为核心活动？

options.en: Hanukkah and Kwanzaa
Holi and Christmas
Ramadan and Holi
Chinese New Year and New Year

explain.en: The book shows Hanukkah lights a menorah and Kwanzaa lights a kinara; the others are not centered on candles.
explain.zh: 书中写明光明节点燃 menorah、宽扎节点燃 kinara，其余节日并非以蜡烛为核心。

q.en: On New Year's Eve, what do many people do to greet the new year?
q.zh: 在新年前夜，许多人会做什么来迎接新年？

options.en: Light nine candles at home
Carry lanterns and sing at dawn according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 提着灯笼在黎明时歌唱（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Stay up until midnight for fireworks and songs
options.zh: 守夜到午夜，放烟花、唱歌曲

options.en: Sleep early and fast all day

explain.en: On New Year's Eve many people stay up until midnight and greet the new year with fireworks, shouting, and songs.
explain.zh: 新年前夜，许多人守夜到午夜，以烟花、欢呼和歌声迎接新年。

prompt.en: The book says all holidays, everywhere, celebrate family, friends, sharing, and love. Think of a holiday your own family celebrates. What traditions does it include, and how are they similar to or different from the holidays in the book?
prompt.zh: 书里说世界各地的节日都在庆祝家人、朋友、分享与爱。想想你家庆祝的某个节日：它有哪些传统？与你读到的节日有哪些相同或不同之处？

en: Name the holiday and 1-2 traditions your family follows.
Compare your traditions to a holiday in the book, noting one similarity and one difference.
zh: 说出节日的名字，以及你家遵循的1-2个传统。
把它和书里的一个节日比较，找出一个相同点和一个不同点。
